heading: Key Takeaway
text: During a chemical reaction, the sum of the masses of the reactants and products remains unchanged. This is known as the Law of Conservation of Mass.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: In a pure chemical compound, elements are always present in a definite proportion by mass. This is known as the Law of Definite Proportions.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: An atom is the smallest particle of the element that cannot usually exist independently and retain all its chemical properties.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: A molecule is the smallest particle of an element or a compound capable of independent existence under ordinary conditions. It shows all the properties of the substance.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: A chemical formula of a compound shows its constituent elements and the number of atoms of each combining element.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Clusters of atoms that act as an ion are called polyatomic ions. They carry a fixed charge on them.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The chemical formula of a molecular compound is determined by the valency of each element.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: In ionic compounds, the charge on each ion is used to determine the chemical formula of the compound.
